title: 10 best herbal teas for chapped lips
intro_desc: Herbal teas for chapped lips are infusions made from natural ingredients that help soothe and moisturize dry, cracked lips. These teas typically contain herbs and flowers known for their healing properties, such as chamomile, calendula, and rose petals. The antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds found in these herbs can help repair damaged lip tissue, reduce redness and irritation, and provide long-lasting hydration. By sipping on these soothing teas, individuals can experience relief from chapped lips, leading to improved comfort and confidence in their daily lives. For example, someone who suffers from dry, cracked lips due to cold weather or frequent lip-licking can benefit from regularly drinking herbal teas for chapped lips, ultimately enjoying smoother, healthier lips.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tea helps with chapped lips because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can soothe and heal dry, cracked lips. The herb's anti-inflammatory compounds can reduce redness and swelling, while its moisturizing properties help to lock in hydration and protect the lips from further damage. Additionally, yarrow tea has antimicrobial properties that can help to prevent infection and promote faster healing. Drinking yarrow tea or using it as a lip balm can provide relief from chapped lips and leave them feeling soft and moisturized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Yarrow tea's anti-inflammatory properties, due to the presence of flavonoids, help reduce redness and swelling, providing relief for chapped lips.
Astringent: The astringent properties of yarrow tea, attributed to tannins, help tighten and heal damaged skin on chapped lips.
Antimicrobial: Yarrow tea's antimicrobial properties, owing to the presence of lactones and essential oils, help prevent infection and promote chapped lip healing.
Moisturizing: Yarrow tea acts as a natural moisturizer, containing plant acids and mucilage, which soothe and hydrate chapped lips.
Analgesic: The analgesic effects of yarrow tea, thanks to the presence of alkaloids, help alleviate pain and discomfort associated with chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: Measure out 1-2 teaspoons of dried yarrow flowers and leaves.
Place the herbs in a tea infuser or teapot with a built-in strainer.
Boil 8 ounces of water and pour it over the yarrow in the infuser or teapot.
Let the tea steep for 10-15 minutes to extract the beneficial compounds.
Discard the spent herbs, allow the tea to cool, then use a cotton swab to apply it to chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants, flowers help soothe and heal chapped lips.
Leaves: High in vitamin C and other nutrients, leaves help moisturize and protect lips from environmental stressors.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids, seeds help nourish and repair dry, cracked lips.
Stems: Packed with anti-inflammatory compounds, stems alleviate irritation and promote healing of chapped lips.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow tea helps with chapped lips because it contains mucilage, a substance that becomes slippery and gel-like when mixed with water. This mucilage creates a protective barrier on the lips, locking in moisture and preventing further dryness. The anti-inflammatory properties of marshmallow tea can also soothe irritated and cracked lips, providing relief and promoting healing. Additionally, marshmallow tea has been shown to have emollient properties, helping to soften and smooth the lips, leaving them feeling hydrated and supple. Regular consumption of marshmallow tea can help keep the lips moisturized and healthy, reducing the likelihood of chapping.
remedy_properties: Demulcent property: Marshmallow tea contains mucilage, a substance that forms a protective film on the skin, helping to soothe and hydrate chapped lips.
Emollient property: The high mucilage content in marshmallow tea can soften and smooth chapped lips, providing relief from dryness and cracking.
Anti-inflammatory property: Marshmallow tea contains flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to reduce inflammation, providing comfort to irritated and chapped lips.
Antimicrobial property: The tea has antimicrobial compounds, such as tannins and phenolic acids, that can help prevent infections and promote wound healing in chapped lips.
Hydrating property: Marshmallow tea is rich in polysaccharides, which can attract and retain moisture, keeping chapped lips hydrated and promoting faster healing.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 tablespoons of dried marshmallow root, then bring to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover, and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Remove from heat, add 1 teaspoon of dried chamomile flowers, and let it steep for 5 minutes.
Strain the tea, then mix in 1 teaspoon of honey for added moisture.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to soothe and heal chapped lips from the inside.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in Vitamin A and C, marshmallow leaves help soothe and moisturize chapped lips.
Flowers: Packed with antioxidants, marshmallow flowers aid in healing and protecting chapped lips from environmental damage.
Roots: Containing mucilage, marshmallow roots help to create a protective barrier on chapped lips, promoting faster healing.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, marshmallow seeds help to nourish and rejuvenate dry, chapped lips.
Bark: A natural anti-inflammatory, marshmallow bark can help reduce redness and irritation associated with chapped lips.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tea helps with chapped lips because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that soothe and hydrate dry, cracked lips. The tea is rich in antioxidants, including flavonoids and carotenoids, which protect the lips from environmental stressors and help to repair damaged tissue. Additionally, pot marigold tea has emollient properties, which means it creates a barrier on the lips to lock in moisture and prevent further dryness. Drinking the tea can also help to promote overall skin health, leading to softer, smoother lips. Regular use of pot marigold tea can provide relief from chapped lips and leave them feeling nourished and rejuvenated.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Pot marigold tea helps reduce inflammation and irritation in chapped lips, thanks to its active constituents like flavonoids and carotenoids.
Moisturizing: The tea has emollient properties that help lock in moisture, keeping lips hydrated and supple, due to its high mucilage content.
Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tea inhibits the growth of bacteria and fungi, preventing infections in cracked lips, as a result of its antimicrobial compounds like flavonoids and essential oils.
Wound healing: Its active constituents, such as triterpenoids, promote tissue regeneration and help repair damaged lips, making it ideal for treating chapped lips.

Antioxidant: Pot marigold tea contains antioxidants like vitamin C and carotenoids, which protect lips from environmental stressors and free radicals, reducing the signs of aging and promoting overall lip health.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, pour 1 cup of boiling water over 1 tablespoon of dried marigold flowers.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10 minutes to extract the herbal properties.
Strain the liquid to remove the marigold flowers, leaving behind the tea.
Let the tea cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Sip the marigold tea slowly, allowing it to moisturize your chapped lips from the inside.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory properties, making them ideal for soothing and healing chapped lips.
Leaves: Contain high levels of vitamin C and antioxidants, helping to repair and protect dry, cracked lips.
Seeds: Packed with essential fatty acids and antioxidants, providing intense moisturizing and nourishment for chapped lips.
Buds: High in anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial compounds, aiding in the prevention and treatment of lip irritations and infections.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Roman chamomile tea helps with chapped lips because it has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. Chamomile is rich in antioxidants that help soothe and heal dry, cracked lips. The tea can be applied directly to the lips or used as a compress to provide instant relief and hydration. Additionally, drinking chamomile tea can help keep the body hydrated from the inside out, promoting overall lip health. Its calming effects can also reduce any discomfort or pain associated with chapped lips. Regular use of roman chamomile tea can help prevent and treat chapped lips, leaving them soft, smooth, and moisturized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Roman chamomile tea has anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce swelling and redness, important for chapped lips as inflammation can worsen dryness and discomfort. This property is attributed to the presence of flavonoids and terpenoids.
Moisturizing: Roman chamomile tea contains compounds that help to lock in moisture, keeping the lips hydrated and preventing further chapping. This is due to the presence of tannins and polyphenols.
Wound-healing: The tea promotes wound healing, which can help to repair damaged lip tissue. This is attributed to the presence of bisabolol, a compound known for its healing and soothing effects.

Antioxidant: Roman chamomile tea has antioxidant properties that help protect the lips from environmental stressors and free radicals, which can cause dryness and chapping. This is due to the high levels of flavonoids and terpenoids.

Analgesic: The tea has analgesic properties that can help alleviate the pain and discomfort associated with chapped lips. This is attributed to the presence of several compounds, including bisabolol and chamazulene.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, pour 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil, which takes about 5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried Roman chamomile flowers, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
After simmering, strain the tea to remove the chamomile flowers, and let it cool down.
Soak a clean cloth in the cooled chamomile tea, then apply the cloth to your chapped lips for 10 minutes.
Discard the remaining tea and store the cloth in a clean, dry place for reuse.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in azulene, which has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and heal chapped lips.
Leaves: Contain flavonoids and essential oils with emollient properties, helping to moisturize and protect chapped lips.
Stems: Have astringent properties that can help dry out any excess moisture, preventing further chapping.
Seeds: Contain lipid-rich oils that can nourish and protect chapped lips, providing long-lasting hydration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps with chapped lips because it contains anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can soothe and hydrate dry, cracked lips. The tea is rich in antioxidants and nutrients that can help to repair and protect the delicate skin on and around the lips, while also promoting blood flow and tissue regeneration. Drinking the tea can help to keep the body hydrated from the inside out, while applying the tea topically as a compress or lip balm can provide direct relief and nourishment to chapped lips. Regular consumption and use of eastern purple coneflower tea can help to prevent and treat chapped lips, leaving the lips soft, smooth, and moisturized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Eastern purple coneflower tea helps reduce inflammation, which is important for chapped lips as it can soothe irritation and redness. The active constituents responsible for this property are mainly chrysins and chrysoeriols.

Moisturizing: The tea has moisturizing properties that help keep the lips hydrated, preventing and healing chapped lips. This is due to the presence of mucilage, a substance that forms a protective layer on the lips, retaining moisture.

Antioxidant: Eastern purple coneflower tea contains antioxidants that help protect the lips from environmental stressors, such as harsh weather conditions, which can contribute to chapped lips. The antioxidant properties come from phenolic compounds, including caffeic acid and chlorogenic acid.

Antimicrobial: The tea exhibits antimicrobial properties, which are important for chapped lips as they can help prevent infection in cracked or damaged lips. The primary active constituents responsible for this property are essential oils and alkamides.

Wound healing: Eastern purple coneflower tea promotes wound healing, which is crucial for chapped lips as it can help repair the damaged lip tissue. This is attributed to echinacea polysaccharides, which stimulate immune cells and enhance tissue regeneration.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 2 cups of water and 2 tablespoons of dried eastern purple coneflower flowers, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, let it simmer for 15 minutes, and allow the coneflower's medicinal properties to infuse into the water.
Strain the tea to remove the solid plant parts, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Add raw honey to taste, stirring until it is fully dissolved in the tea.
Drink 1-2 cups of this herbal tea daily, as its nourishing and moisturizing properties can help soothe and heal chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and essential oils, the flowers of the eastern purple coneflower are used in medicinal teas for chapped lips due to their soothing and anti-inflammatory properties.
Leaves: High in tannins and antioxidants, the leaves help create medicinal teas that provide relief and healing for chapped lips.
Buds: Packed with vitamins and minerals, coneflower buds contribute to medicinal teas that nourish and protect chapped lips.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids, the seeds are used in medicinal teas for chapped lips to moisturize and support skin regeneration.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tea helps with chapped lips because it has anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and heal cracked lips. The tea is also rich in vitamins and minerals that can moisturize and nourish the lips, preventing them from becoming dry and chapped. Moreover, fennel tea has antioxidant properties that can protect the lips from environmental stressors that can cause chapping. Drinking fennel tea can also help keep the body hydrated, which is essential for maintaining soft and supple lips. Regular use of fennel tea can provide long-term relief from chapped lips and keep them healthy and moisturized.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Fennel tea helps reduce inflammation and irritation in chapped lips, thanks to the presence of rosmarinic acid and flavonoids.
Moisturizing: The emollient properties of fennel tea help to lock in moisture, keeping lips hydrated and preventing further chapping, due to the essential oils and tannins found in fennel.
Antioxidant: Fennel tea contains antioxidants such as vitamin C, which can help protect lips from environmental stressors and promote healing, reducing the appearance of chapped lips.

Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties of fennel tea help prevent infections and promote wound healing, important for treating chapped lips that may have developed cracks or sores, thanks to the presence of volatile oils like trans-anethole.
Analgesic: Fennel tea has a mild analgesic effect, helping to alleviate pain and discomfort associated with chapped lips, due to the presence of rosmarinic acid and certain flavonoids.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, boil 2 cups of water and add 2 teaspoons of crushed fennel seeds, letting it simmer for 10 minutes.
Turn off the heat, cover the pot, and allow the fennel tea to steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the fennel tea to remove the seeds, pouring it into a heat-resistant container.
Allow the fennel tea to cool down to a warm temperature that is comfortable for applying to your lips.
Soak a clean washcloth in the fennel tea, then hold it against your chapped lips for 5-10 minutes, repeating this process 2-3 times a day.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Rich in vitamins and antioxidants, fennel leaves can help soothe and moisturize chapped lips.
Flowers: Containing essential oils and flavonoids, fennel flowers can provide anti-inflammatory and emollient properties for lip care.
Seeds: High in fatty acids and antioxidants, fennel seeds can help repair and protect chapped lips from environmental stressors.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tea helps with chapped lips because it contains a compound called glycyrrhizin, which has anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties. This compound helps to soothe and heal dry, cracked lips by reducing inflammation and providing hydration. Additionally, licorice tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the lips from environmental stressors that can contribute to chapping. Drinking licorice tea or using it as a lip balm can provide relief and help keep lips soft, smooth, and healthy.
remedy_properties: Moisturizing: Licorice tea is rich in glycyrrhizic acid, which helps to retain moisture in the skin, providing relief to chapped lips.
Anti-inflammatory: The flavonoids and coumarins present in licorice tea exhibit anti-inflammatory properties, reducing irritation and swelling in chapped lips.
Antioxidant: Licorice tea contains powerful antioxidants, such as liquiritigenin and isoliquiritigenin, which help protect the lips from damage caused by free radicals and environmental factors.
Antimicrobial: Licorice tea has antimicrobial properties due to its saponin and flavonoid content, helping to prevent infections and promote wound healing in chapped lips.
Soothing: Licorice tea's demulcent effect, attributed to its mucilage content, forms a protective layer on the lips, providing a soothing and calming effect on chapped lips.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 teaspoons of dried licorice root; bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Remove the pot from heat, add 1 teaspoon of honey and stir until fully dissolved.
Allow the tea to cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Strain the tea into a cup, discarding the licorice root solids.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to moisten and soothe chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain glycyrrhizin, a substance that helps to soothe and moisturize chapped lips.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to reduce redness and irritation associated with chapped lips.
Flowers: The flowers of the licorice plant contain flavonoids, which have antioxidant properties that can help to protect the lips from environmental stressors that can contribute to chapped lips.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice plant contain essential fatty acids that can help to nourish and hydrate the lips, preventing chapping.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains triterpenoid saponins, which have emollient properties that can help to soften and smooth chapped lips.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Witch hazel tea helps with chapped lips because it contains tannins, which have anti-inflammatory and astringent properties. These properties can help to soothe and heal dry, cracked lips by reducing inflammation and irritation, and by helping to repair damaged skin tissue. Additionally, witch hazel has natural moisturizing properties that can help to hydrate and protect the lips from further damage. Drinking witch hazel tea can also help to keep the body hydrated, which is important for maintaining soft, supple lips. Overall, the soothing, healing, and moisturizing properties of witch hazel tea make it an effective natural remedy for chapped lips.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Witch hazel tea can reduce inflammation and swelling, which is important for chapped lips as it can soothe irritation and promote healing. This property is due to the presence of tannins and volatile oils in witch hazel.

Astringent: Witch hazel tea has a drying effect, which helps to remove excess oil and moisture from chapped lips. This is important because it can help to prevent further cracking and dryness. The astringent properties come from the tannins in witch hazel.

Antioxidant: Witch hazel tea contains antioxidants that can protect the lips from damage caused by free radicals. This is important for chapped lips as it can help to prevent further damage and promote healing. The antioxidant properties are primarily due to the presence of flavonoids in witch hazel.

Antimicrobial: Witch hazel tea has antimicrobial properties that can help to prevent infection in chapped lips. This is important because cracked and broken skin can be more susceptible to infection. The antimicrobial properties are primarily due to the presence of volatile oils in witch hazel.

Emollient: Witch hazel tea can help to lock in moisture and form a protective barrier on the lips. This is important for chapped lips as it can help to prevent further dryness and keep the lips hydrated. The emollient properties are due to the presence of saponins in witch hazel.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, combine 1 cup of water and 2 tablespoons of dried witch hazel leaves, then bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce heat, cover the pot, and let it simmer for 10-15 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the leaves, then let it cool down to a drinkable temperature.
Add raw honey to taste, stirring until it's fully dissolved.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing it to moisturize and soothe chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Bark: The bark of the witch hazel plant is rich in tannins, which have astringent properties that can help soothe and heal chapped lips.
Leaves: The leaves of the witch hazel plant contain flavonoids and essential oils that have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, making them useful for treating chapped lips.
Flowers: The flowers of the witch hazel plant contain phenolic acids and tannins, which have astringent and anti-inflammatory properties that can help heal and soothe chapped lips.
Seeds: The seeds of the witch hazel plant contain oils that are high in antioxidants, making them useful for moisturizing and protecting chapped lips.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of the witch hazel plant contain a high concentration of tannins, which have astringent properties that can help heal and soothe chapped lips.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tea helps with chapped lips because it contains anti-inflammatory and healing properties that can soothe and repair damaged skin. The tea is rich in antioxidants, which can help protect the lips from environmental stressors that can cause dryness and cracking. Additionally, St. John's wort has been shown to increase the production of collagen, a protein that helps keep the skin supple and moisturized. Drinking the tea can also help hydrate the body from the inside out, keeping the lips hydrated and preventing chapping. Regular consumption of St. John's wort tea can help keep the lips soft, smooth, and healthy.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: St. John's wort tea contains hypericin and hyperforin, which help reduce inflammation, providing relief to chapped and irritated lips.
Wound healing: The plant's antibacterial and antiviral properties, due to hypericin and hyperforin, promote wound healing, aiding in the recovery of damaged lip tissue.
Moisturizing: St. John's wort tea contains tannins and flavonoids that have astringent properties, helping to moisturize and soothe dry, chapped lips.
Pain relief: The tea's analgesic properties, attributed to hypericin and hyperforin, help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by chapped lips.
Antioxidant: St. John's wort tea is rich in antioxidants, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, that protect lips from environmental stressors and support overall skin health.
remedy_recipe: In a teapot, combine 1 teaspoon of dried St. John's wort flowers with 8 ounces of boiling water.
Allow the mixture to steep for 10 minutes to extract the herb's beneficial compounds.
Strain the tea to remove the spent flowers, leaving only the liquid.
Let the tea cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing the hydrating and soothing properties of St. John's wort to help relieve chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in flavonoids and volatile oils, the flowers of St. John's wort are useful in making medicinal teas for chapped lips due to their anti-inflammatory and soothing properties.
Leaves: High in hypericin and hyperforin, St. John's wort leaves are used in teas to help heal chapped lips, as they have been found to have wound-healing and skin-protecting effects.
Stems: Containing similar compounds as leaves, stems of St. John's wort can also be used in teas for chapped lips, offering soothing and skin-regenerating benefits.
Rhizomes: Packed with bioactive compounds, rhizomes of St. John's wort are used in medicinal teas for chapped lips, thanks to their astringent and antimicrobial properties.
Buds: Buds of St. John's wort are rich in antioxidants and can be used in teas for chapped lips, providing protective and rejuvenating effects.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tea helps with chapped lips because it contains anti-inflammatory properties that can soothe and heal dry, cracked lips. The herbal tea is rich in antioxidants that can help to repair damaged skin cells and promote the growth of new, healthy cells. Additionally, lavender has moisturizing properties that can hydrate and soften chapped lips, providing relief from discomfort and preventing further damage. Drinking lavender tea can also help to keep the body hydrated, which is essential for maintaining soft and supple lips. Regularly consuming lavender tea and using it as a lip balm can help to keep chapped lips at bay, providing a natural and chemical-free solution to this common problem.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: English lavender tea contains compounds like linalyl acetate and linalool that help reduce inflammation, which is important for chapped lips as it helps soothe irritation and redness.
Moisturizing: Lavender tea has emollient properties, which help to lock in moisture and keep the lips hydrated, thus reducing the chances of chapping.
Antimicrobial: English lavender tea has antimicrobial properties due to the presence of compounds like eucalyptol and camphor, which help protect chapped lips from infections and promote healing.
Analgesic: The soothing properties of lavender tea, due to compounds like linalool and caryophyllene, help alleviate pain and discomfort caused by chapped lips.

Wound healing: English lavender tea contains compounds like tannins and flavonoids that promote tissue repair and regeneration, which is crucial for healing chapped lips and preventing further damage.
remedy_recipe: In a pot, pour 1 cup of water and bring it to a boil, which takes approximately 5 minutes.
Add 1 teaspoon of dried English lavender flowers to the boiling water, then lower the heat and let it simmer for 10 minutes.
Turn off the heat and let the lavender tea steep for an additional 10 minutes.
Strain the tea to remove the lavender flowers, then let it cool to a comfortable drinking temperature.
Sip the tea slowly, allowing the moisture to soothe and heal chapped lips.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, lavender flowers help soothe and heal chapped lips.
Leaves: Containing oils with moisturizing and analgesic properties, lavender leaves help relieve and protect dry, cracked lips.
Stems: With astringent properties, lavender stems can help tighten and protect the skin on lips, reducing the appearance of chapping.
Seeds: High in essential fatty acids, lavender seeds can help nourish and rejuvenate dry, damaged lips.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat chapped lips with herbal teas is to brew a strong tea using chamomile or calendula flowers. Allow the tea to cool, then gently apply it to your lips with a cotton swab. The anti-inflammatory properties of these herbs can help soothe and heal cracked lips. For added moisture, mix in a small amount of honey or coconut oil before applying. Repeat this treatment several times a day, and remember to also stay hydrated by drinking plenty of fluids.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to chapped lips that are treated with herbal teas include dry, irritated, or inflamed skin. Certain herbal teas have anti-inflammatory and moisturizing properties that can soothe and heal skin. For example, chamomile tea can be used as a compress to reduce redness and irritation, while calendula tea can be applied topically to promote wound healing and hydration. Other herbal teas, such as lavender and rosehip, also have soothing and anti-inflammatory effects that can help alleviate symptoms of chapped lips and other skin ailments. However, it is important to note that individual responses to herbal remedies may vary, and it is always best to consult with a healthcare provider before using any new treatments.
